# DevOps / Site Reliability Engineer

Own the infrastructure and developer-experience platform for our product
engineering team. You'll build the paved road that lets the rest of the
team ship safely and fast.

## What you'll do

- Design and operate our AWS infrastructure: VPC, ECS, RDS, S3, IAM,
  managed via Terraform. Production reliability is your bottom line.
- Own the CI/CD pipeline. Builds should be fast, deterministic, and safe.
  GitHub Actions for build, ArgoCD for deploy.
- Run the observability stack: Datadog dashboards, log pipelines, alert
  routing, SLO/SLI definitions.
- Lead the on-call rotation: define playbooks, run incident response,
  drive postmortems to root-cause fixes.
- Build self-serve tooling for product engineers: log access, secret
  rotation, environment provisioning, rollback. Reduce the "ask DevOps"
  surface area.
- Champion deployment safety: progressive rollouts, feature flags,
  canary analysis.
- Partner with backend engineers on schema migrations, scaling, and
  cost management.

## What you bring

- 5+ years infrastructure / SRE work. Production-incident scars optional
  but appreciated.
- Strong Terraform — modules you'd be proud of, not copy-paste.
- AWS depth: networking, IAM, ECS or EKS, RDS, S3 lifecycle, cost
  attribution.
- CI/CD: GitHub Actions or comparable. You've debugged a flaky build
  pipeline at 11pm.
- Observability instinct: when something breaks, you reach for the
  dashboard before the logs.
- Comfort writing Python or Go for tooling. Bash is fine for glue;
  past a hundred lines, write real code.
- Code review and testing discipline — Terraform modules get reviewed
  too.

## Stack

AWS, Terraform, GitHub Actions, ArgoCD, Datadog, PagerDuty, Postgres,
Redis, Python tooling.
